body:
A private residential commission to build a recirculating sandstone waterfall as the centrepiece of a sloping Canterbury backyard. The brief asked for something that felt like it had always been there — bushrock sourced from a single quarry run, set so the water reads as a natural seep rather than a feature.

Profile Landscapes handled the full scope: excavation and retaining, the concealed pump chamber and balance tank, all stonework, and a surrounding planting of ferns, lomandra and dwarf lillypilly. The pondless design recirculates through a hidden reservoir, so there is no open water to maintain or fence.

Delivered over nine weeks with our own crew and stone from our regular Hawkesbury supplier.

Northmead Civic Plaza is a public realm upgrade for Blacktown City Council, replacing a tired paved forecourt with a resilient, low-water landscape built around massed ornamental grasses and shade trees.

The package covered demolition of the existing hardstand, new permeable paving, raised planters, integrated seating and a 1,400-square-metre planting of Lomandra, Poa and Dianella under a canopy of Angophora. Plant material was grown to order at our Petersham nursery to guarantee availability and consistency across the whole site.

Delivered under a Council civil contract with full WHS and traffic management, completed across an 18-week programme.

A complete rear-garden transformation for a Woollahra residence, combining a new concrete pool surround, a hardwood pergola and a layered subtropical planting scheme.

The scope ran from bulk excavation and drainage through to the spotted-gum pergola, travertine paving, built-in bench seating and a planting of frangipani, strelitzia and clumping bamboo for screening. Profile Landscapes coordinated the pool shell installation and self-performed all surrounding hard and soft works.

Delivered over twelve weeks with our own carpentry and landscape crews.

A tropical garden and timber boardwalk for a Hunters Hill residence on a steep, damp site running down to a rear reserve. The boardwalk solves access across boggy ground while creating a walk through a layered subtropical planting.

Profile Landscapes built the elevated hardwood boardwalk on screw-pile footings, installed sub-surface drainage, and planted a canopy-to-groundcover scheme of palms, gingers, bromeliads and tree ferns. The result reads as a rainforest walk that thrives in conditions most gardens would struggle with.

Delivered over eleven weeks.

The brief called for a civic-quality landscape within a tight programme on one of Sydney busiest commercial addresses. Profile Landscapes delivered 2,400 square metres of planted terracing, integrated seating and stone paving across six levels of the heritage-listed facade.

Plant selection was drawn from our in-house encyclopedia: Allocasuarina torulosa for canopy, Westringia species for structure, and a carefully tuned palette of ornamental grasses for texture and movement at eye level.

The project was delivered under Lendlease as principal contractor, with a compressed 18-week site programme managed through our in-house estimating platform.

A heritage-sensitive softscape package for the City of Sydney within the Martin Place precinct. The work required planting and fine finishes around significant heritage stonework, with strict controls on access, noise and protection of existing fabric.

Profile Landscapes delivered raised planters, sub-surface irrigation, semi-mature tree installation by crane out of hours, and a refined groundcover palette selected to suit the precinct's formal character. Every plant was sourced and held at our nursery so the install could hit narrow night-works windows without supply risk.

Completed in stages around full public access to one of Sydney's busiest civic spaces.

A schoolyard renewal for Chifley Public School under the NSW Department of Education, turning an underused asphalt yard into a green, shaded play and learning environment.

The package included shade-tree planting, soft-fall play zones, an outdoor classroom with tiered seating, raised vegetable garden beds and a sensory planting trail. All works were programmed around the school calendar and delivered to DoE safety and compliance standards, with the bulk of disruptive works completed in the summer holidays.

Plant stock was grown at our nursery to meet the tight holiday-period install window.

A compact heritage courtyard in Glebe, restored and replanted for a private owner. The terrace's original bluestone was lifted, cleaned and relaid, with new sub-surface drainage to resolve long-standing damp issues against the heritage wall.

Profile Landscapes added a wall-mounted water feature, espaliered citrus, and a shade-tolerant planting of clivia, hellebore and tree fern suited to the courtyard's southern aspect. Small site, tight access — all material was barrowed through the house.

A precise, low-volume job delivered by a two-person heritage crew over five weeks.

Three adjoining Camperdown terrace gardens delivered as a single coordinated package for a private owner refurbishing all three properties. A shared design language ties the gardens together while each retains its own character.

The work covered new rear decks, recycled-brick paving, integrated planters and a cohesive planting palette of natives and edibles across all three. Delivering the trio together let us share access, plant and crew — bringing the per-garden cost well below three separate jobs.

Completed over ten weeks with one continuous crew.

A second-stage tropical planting and path renewal for a Hunters Hill property, extending an earlier boardwalk commission into the lower garden.

The package added a crushed-sandstone path network, additional sub-surface drainage and a deepened subtropical planting of palms, philodendron and clumping bamboo. Continuity of crew and plant supply from the first stage kept the new work seamless with the existing garden.

Completed over eight weeks.

A waterfall and palm garden for a Neutral Bay residence, built into a steep harbourside slope. The recirculating water feature steps down the grade through sandstone boulders, framed by a canopy of mature palms.

Profile Landscapes delivered the retaining, the pondless waterfall system, feature lighting and a planting of Bangalow palm, bird's-nest fern and bromeliad underplanting. Access was by a single narrow side path, so all stone and plant material was craned over the house.

Completed over nine weeks.

A therapeutic healing garden for Western Sydney Local Health District at Westmead Hospital, designed to give patients, families and staff a calm outdoor space within the hospital campus.

The package covered accessible pathways, raised sensory planters, shaded seating arbours and a planting scheme chosen for year-round interest, fragrance and low allergen load. All works met healthcare-grade infection-control, access and safety requirements, and were staged around continuous hospital operation.

Plant material was grown and held at our nursery to suit the controlled install programme.
